content.text:
Our CI on `main` is red. The test `TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount` in the `payments` package is failing. Please find the root cause and fix it.

Hard constraint: the test is correct — do NOT edit the test or change its expected values. Fix the production code so the test passes.
I'll reproduce the failure first, then trace the discount logic from there.

content.content:
=== RUN   TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount
    checkout_test.go:14: discount not applied: want total $90.00 (10% off 100.00), got $100.00
--- FAIL: TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount (0.00s)
FAIL
FAIL	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.011s

content.text: The 10% discount isn't being subtracted from the total. Let me find everywhere discounts are referenced in the package.
checkout.go computes `discounted` on line 55 but the receipt on line 61 returns `order.Subtotal`. Let me read `ApplyDiscount` to confirm the helper itself is correct.
`ApplyDiscount` is correct — a 10% discount returns 90% of the subtotal. So the bug is in checkout.go, where the discounted value is computed but never used. Let me read that function.

Confirmed the root cause. `discounted := ApplyDiscount(...)` is computed and then thrown away (`_ = discounted`), and the receipt charges the full `order.Subtotal`. I'll return the discounted total instead.

input.new_string:
discounted := ApplyDiscount(order.Subtotal, order.DiscountPct)

	return Receipt{Total: discounted, Lines: lines}

content.content: The file payments/checkout.go has been updated successfully.

content.text: Edit applied. Re-running the previously failing test.

content.content:
=== RUN   TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount
--- PASS: TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount (0.00s)
PASS
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.009s

content.text: Passing. Running the full package to check for regressions.

content.content: ok  	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.214s

content.text:
Done with the first issue.

**Root cause:** `payments/checkout.go` computed the discounted total (`discounted := ApplyDiscount(...)`) but discarded it with `_ = discounted` and returned `Receipt{Total: order.Subtotal}` — so the full pre-discount subtotal was charged. `ApplyDiscount` itself was correct.

**Fix:** return `Total: discounted`. One-line change in `Checkout`; the test was untouched per your constraint. `TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount` passes and the full `payments` package is green.

Perfect. Two follow-ups:
1. Add a regression test for the discount so CI catches this if it ever regresses again — ideally a multi-item order so it's a stronger case than the existing single-item one.
2. Finance flagged that some multi-line orders show a total that's off by a cent or two versus what they expect. Please investigate and fix if it's a real bug.

Same constraint as before: don't weaken any existing test.
I'll add the regression test first, matching the existing test style, then dig into the rounding report. Let me read the current test file.
I'll add a multi-item discounted-order case (25% off a $200 two-item order → $150) after the existing tests, mirroring their table-free style.

content.content: The file payments/checkout_test.go has been updated successfully.

content.text: Running the discount tests to confirm the new case passes.

content.content:
=== RUN   TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount
--- PASS: TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount (0.00s)
=== RUN   TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount_MultiItem
--- PASS: TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount_MultiItem (0.00s)
PASS
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.010s

content.text: Regression test is in and green. Now the off-by-a-cent report. Totals are `Money` (integer cents), so a drift usually comes from rounding — most often tax or per-line math. Let me find where the receipt total is computed and where tax is applied.
Two suspects: `MulPercent` (the rounding primitive) and `ApplyTax`, which from the grep taxes each line and sums. Let me read `MulPercent` first to see how it rounds.

`MulPercent` rounds correctly (round-half-up to the nearest cent), so the primitive is fine. That points at `ApplyTax` rounding *each line* and summing — the classic per-line rounding drift. Let me read it.

That's the bug. `ApplyTax` rounds **each line** to the nearest cent and then sums, so every line's sub-cent remainder is rounded independently and the errors accumulate. Taxing the summed subtotal once and rounding a single time gives a different (correct) total. Let me prove it with a focused reproduction test before changing anything — three $3.33 lines at 8% tax.

content.content: File created successfully at payments/tax_test.go

content.text: Running the reproduction.

content.content:
=== RUN   TestApplyTax_NoPerLineDrift
    tax_test.go:18: tax total drifted: want $10.79, got $10.80
--- FAIL: TestApplyTax_NoPerLineDrift (0.00s)
FAIL
FAIL	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.008s

content.text: Reproduced exactly — the per-line rounding drifts a cent high ($10.80 vs the correct $10.79). The fix is to tax the order subtotal once and round a single time. I'll keep line `Amount`s pre-tax (the per-line tax display wasn't what finance flagged, and per-line tax is precisely where the drift came from) and compute the taxed total from the summed subtotal.

content.content: The file payments/tax.go has been updated successfully.

content.text: Running the tax tests to confirm the drift is gone.

content.content:
=== RUN   TestApplyTax_NoPerLineDrift
--- PASS: TestApplyTax_NoPerLineDrift (0.00s)
PASS
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.009s

content.text: Before I move on, I want to be sure `ApplyTax` was the only place doing per-line rounding and that nothing else recomputes an order total a different way. Let me widen the search across the whole repo.
There's a call site in `orders/summary.go` that calls `payments.ApplyTax` and reads `Total`. Let me confirm it consumes the returned total rather than recomputing tax itself.

Good — it routes through `Checkout` + `ApplyTax` and just uses `receipt.Total`, so it inherits the fix; no separate rounding there. Last check: the shared types, to confirm `Receipt.Total` is the single total field everything reads.

Confirmed: `Receipt.Total` is the only total field, and every path — `Checkout`, `ApplyTax`, and the `orders` summary — flows through it. The tax fix is complete and localized; nothing else does per-line rounding.
Great work. Run the full test suite to make sure nothing else regressed, then give me a concise summary of everything you changed.
Running the entire suite across all packages.

content.content:
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/payments	0.231s
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/orders	0.142s
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/api	0.310s
ok  	github.com/acme/shop/internal/config	0.044s

content.text:
All packages pass. Summary of everything I changed:

**1. Discount not applied (the original CI failure)**
- File: `payments/checkout.go`, `Checkout()`.
- Root cause: the discounted total was computed (`discounted := ApplyDiscount(...)`) then discarded (`_ = discounted`); the receipt returned `order.Subtotal`.
- Fix: return `Total: discounted`. `ApplyDiscount` was already correct.

**2. Regression test**
- File: `payments/checkout_test.go`.
- Added `TestCheckout_AppliesDiscount_MultiItem` (25% off a $200 two-item order → $150) so this can't silently regress. Existing tests were left unchanged.

**3. Off-by-a-cent total (finance report)**
- File: `payments/tax.go`, `ApplyTax()`.
- Root cause: tax was applied and rounded **per line**, then summed, so each line's sub-cent remainder was rounded independently and the errors accumulated (e.g. three $3.33 lines totalled $10.80 instead of $10.79).
- Fix: sum the pre-tax line amounts and tax the subtotal once, rounding a single time. Added `payments/tax_test.go:TestApplyTax_NoPerLineDrift` capturing the exact case.

**Verification:** `go test ./...` is green across all four packages. The discount fix, its regression test, and the tax fix with its reproduction test are all in place. Nothing else regressed.
